name: SYNOVIAL OSTEOCHONDROMATOSIS
definition: SYNOVIAL CHONDROMATOSIS = JOINT CHONDROMA benign self-limiting monoarticular disorder characterized by proliferation + metaplastic joint surface disintegration

demographics.Histo: foci of hyaline cartilage with mineralized chondroid matrix beneath synovial surface

distribution: peripheral contrast enhancement of chondral lesions
discriminating_features: pathognomonic multiple calcified / ossified loose bodies in a single joint (bony shell of
characteristically uniform size of nodules that may vary between a few mm and several cm
varying degrees of bone mineralization (25-30% of chondromas show no radiopacity)
marginal pressure erosion of adjacent bone in joints with tight capsule (eg, hip)
widening of joint space (from accumulation of loose bodies)

MR: homogeneous lobulated intraarticular mass isointense to muscle on T1WI + hyperintense

clinical.Rx: surgical synovectomy with removal of loose bodies (recurrence is common)
